content.problemStatement:
In a cricket game, an over is a set of six valid deliveries of balls performed by one player ― the bowler for this over.

Consider a cricket game with a series of NNN overs (numbered 111 through NNN) played by KKK players (numbered 111 through KKK). Each player may be the bowler for at most LLL overs in total, but the same player may not be the bowler for any two consecutive overs. Assign exactly one bowler to each over in such a way that these rules are satisfied or determine that no such assignment exists.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated integers N, K and L.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line. If no valid assignment exists print -1. Otherwise print N space‑separated integers where the i‑th integer is the player number (1‑based) bowling the i‑th over.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test case: round‑robin gives 1 2 3 1 which respects all limits. Second test case: total capacity K·L = 4 < N = 5, so impossible.

solution.approach: The only restrictions are (1) each player bowls at most L overs and (2) the same player cannot appear in two consecutive overs. If K == 1 then we can only bowl a single over, otherwise we need N <= K * L (total capacity). When these conditions hold we can simply assign bowlers in a round‑robin order: for i from 0 to N‑1 assign player (i mod K) + 1. This guarantees no two consecutive overs have the same bowler and each player gets either floor(N/K) or ceil(N/K) overs, which is never larger than L because N <= K*L implies ceil(N/K) <= L. The construction runs in O(N) time per test case and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(T * N) overall.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it)); K = int(next(it)); L = int(next(it))
        if N == 1:
            out_lines.append('1')
            continue
        if K == 1 or K * L < N:
            out_lines.append('-1')
            continue
        seq = [(i % K) + 1 for i in range(N)]
        out_lines.append(' '.join(map(str, seq)))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
